SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.expiry

Class ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.expiry.ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the payload type whose expiry the policy governs
public final class ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T> extends Object
Reusable test harness that checks a custom ExpiryPolicy against the contract every implementation must honour. SPI implementors drop this into a unit test and call verify() to catch the common mistakes — a null expiry, an isExpired that ignores the stored expireOn, or a computeExpiry that returns an already-past instant.

This class is dependency-free (no test library on the classpath) and throws AssertionError on the first violation, so it composes with JUnit, TestNG, or a plain main.

Usage


 @Test
 void honoursTheExpiryContract() {
     Failover failover = ...;            // a mock/stub configured with your duration + unit
     ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier.forPolicy(myPolicy)
             .withFailover(failover)
             .withSamplePayload(new Country("FR"))
             .verify();
 }

The harness verifies the standard expireOn-based contract: isExpired is driven by the payload's expireOn field. Policies that derive expiry from a payload field instead (payload-driven) should test that bespoke logic directly — call verifyComputeExpiry() for the computeExpiry checks only.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static <T> ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T>
    forPolicy(ExpiryPolicy<T> policy)
    Starts a verification for the given policy.
    void
    verify()
    Runs the full standard contract: computeExpiry checks plus the expireOn-based isExpired checks.
    Instant
    verifyComputeExpiry()
    Verifies only the computeExpiry contract and returns the computed instant: it must be non-null and strictly in the future (a freshly computed expiry is never already past).
    ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T>
    withFailover(Failover failover)
    The Failover metadata passed to the policy — typically a Mockito mock with the duration/unit stubbed.
    ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T>
    withSamplePayload(T samplePayload)
    A representative payload instance stored in the synthetic ReferentialPayload used for the checks.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • forPolicy

      public static <T> ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T> forPolicy(ExpiryPolicy<T> policy)
      Starts a verification for the given policy.

    • withFailover

      public ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T> withFailover(Failover failover)
      The Failover metadata passed to the policy — typically a Mockito mock with the duration/unit stubbed.

    • withSamplePayload

      public ExpiryPolicyContractVerifier<T> withSamplePayload(T samplePayload)
      A representative payload instance stored in the synthetic ReferentialPayload used for the checks.

    • verify

      public void verify()
      Runs the full standard contract: computeExpiry checks plus the expireOn-based isExpired checks.

    • verifyComputeExpiry

      public Instant verifyComputeExpiry()
      Verifies only the computeExpiry contract and returns the computed instant: it must be non-null and strictly in the future (a freshly computed expiry is never already past).
